rulings.Case Title: STUART E MOLL REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST AND RESTATED INTERVIVOS TRUST AGREEMENT OF STUART E MOLL
rulings.Calendar Matter: S.J. Del Montes'S, As Executor Fo The Estate Of S. Moll, Notice Of Motion Anf Motion To Strike All Claims For Punitive Or Exemplary Damages Against Decedent For The Sixth Amended Verified Petition To Establish And Enforce Trust, Ect. And Mem. Of Pts. And Auth. In Support Thereof.
rulings.Rulings: Hearing required: (1) Does Colorado law apply a presumption of revocability and, if so, is that limited to assets contributed by the surviving settlor? (2) If the Court rejects the various procedural challenges on demurrer, but concludes that Petitioners have not pleaded facts supporting an enforceable contract not to revoke or alter the Trust¿s terms, which causes of action would survive against which Respondents? How would that be affected if the Court concluded that the Trust was revocable as a matter or law? Kenneth¿s demurrer, which stands unopposed, is sustained without leave to amend. The motion to strike punitive damages claims against the decedent is granted.

rulings.Case Title: STUART E MOLL REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST AND RESTATED INTERVIVOS TRUST AGREEMENT OF STUART E MOLL
rulings.Calendar Matter: General Demurrer By Respondent, Kenneth P. Del Monte, To Sixth Amended Verified Petition To Establish And Enforce Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing required: (1) Does Colorado law apply a presumption of revocability and, if so, is that limited to assets contributed by the surviving settlor? (2) If the Court rejects the various procedural challenges on demurrer, but concludes that Petitioners have not pleaded facts supporting an enforceable contract not to revoke or alter the Trust¿s terms, which causes of action would survive against which Respondents? How would that be affected if the Court concluded that the Trust was revocable as a matter or law? Kenneth¿s demurrer, which stands unopposed, is sustained without leave to amend. The motion to strike punitive damages claims against the decedent is granted.

rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL BRIMER VS. OFFICE DEPOT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Intention To Move For New Trial
rulings.Rulings: Denied. A new trial is not proper where there has only been summary adjudication of affirmative defenses. In any event, the motion reargues issues already considered by the Court and does not present any basis for the Court to conclude that a new trial is appropriate. As explained at the hearing, the Court has not adjudicated any element of Plaintiff¿s case-in-chief or precluded any evidence or argument Defendant might seek to present to show that Plaintiff cannot carry its burden.
